title: rootless: "The cgroups manager is set to systemd blah blah"
body:
#4574 is back in podman-1.8.1-0.3.rc1.fc31 (and, I believe, in rawhide)
```console
$ podman ps
WARN[0000] The cgroups manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root)
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
```
Yes, linger is enabled. Error goes away if I manually set magic envariables:
```
$ export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000
$ export DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS="unix:path=${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/bus"
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
```

comments.body: Are those environment variables not set otherwise?
No, but podman has (had?) some sort of magic internal logic that set them to defaults if unset.
Did this part of the rootless code move into containers/common? Potentially we desynced between common and Podman implementations?

No, and that's what's stumping me! I don't see anywhere that could be affecting this:
```console
$ git cdif 84640525 c8436cd8
...long output, but only one place touches XDG_etc, and I can't see how that would break anything
```
The DBUS-setting code is in `libpod/runtime.go:SetXdgDirs()` and I could see how things might get twisty if `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` is unset but the warning happens even if it _is_ set. And `/bus` exists under it. I've even tried stepping back to clear my mind; I don't see what can be causing this.

`ltrace` seems to imply that `SetXdgDirs()` is getting invoked and is performing correctly:
```
881629 setenv("XDG_RUNTIME_DIR", "/run/user/1000", 1)                            = 0
881629 setenv("DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS", "unix:path=/run/user/1000/bus", 1)     = 0
```
(but it could also be the `Setenv` in `vendor/github.com/godbus/dbus/conn.go`. I don't know how to tell).

Update. I'm starting to feel sure that it's something to do with this new file:
```
vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/config/config.go
```
There's a `NewConfig()` that calls `config.checkCgroupsAndAdjustConfig()` which looks suspiciously like the warning we're seeing. If this somehow gets invoked before `SetXdgDirs()` in `cmd/podman/main.go`, it would explain the problem. Can someone go-literate please acknowledge and confirm?
